review_notes: The functions provided are applicable to Kubernetes and align well with the NIST 800-53 IA-4(4) control on identifier management. They focus on preventing excessive privileges, which is a key aspect of identifier management. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but the function names could be slightly improved for clarity. The functions are technically accurate and map to real Kubernetes security controls.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'k8s_rbac_service_account_token_automount_disabled' to 'k8s_rbac_service_account_token_automount_prevented' for clarity in action description.
title: Identifier Management - . ]
step1_reasoning: The control IA-4(4) focuses on identifier management, specifically ensuring no root access keys are used. In Kubernetes, this translates to ensuring that no overly privileged roles are bound to users or service accounts, which could be equivalent to having root access. The functions focus on preventing cluster-admin bindings, enforcing least privilege, and ensuring service account tokens are not automatically mounted, which aligns with the principle of minimizing unnecessary access.
